name: Azerbaijani Qutab with Greens
steps: Make a thin, simple dough from the flour and water.
Fill with the chopped greens and cheese, fold in half, and cook on a hot, dry griddle until browned in spots.
Brush with the oil before serving.
steps_detailed: Make a thin, elastic dough from the flour and water, and roll into a thin round.
Mix the greens with the onion and cheese, spread over half the round, and fold over into a half-moon, sealing the edge.
Cook on a hot, dry griddle, 2-3 minutes per side, until browned in spots and cooked through.
Brush with the oil before serving - qutab, thin stuffed flatbread, is a beloved Azerbaijani breakfast, filled countless ways.

name: Azerbaijani Cheese and Herb Plate
steps: Arrange the cheese, cucumber, and herbs on a plate.
Serve with warm flatbread.
steps_detailed: Arrange the cheese, cucumber, and fresh herbs on a plate.
Serve with warm flatbread for wrapping bites together - a simple, common Azerbaijani breakfast spread.

name: Azerbaijani Sweet Tea with Jam
steps: Brew black tea.
Serve with the preserves on the side, traditionally eaten by the spoonful between sips rather than stirred in.
steps_detailed: Brew black tea, adding a pinch of cardamom if using.
Serve with the preserves on the side - Azerbaijani tea culture calls for eating jam by the spoonful between sips, never stirring it in, a beloved daily ritual.

name: Azerbaijani Dovga

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Simmer the rice in 1.5 cups water until nearly tender, about 12 minutes.
Whisk the yogurt with the egg, then slowly stir into the rice, warming gently without boiling.
Stir in the cilantro and serve.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Simmer the rice and beans in 1.75 cups water until nearly tender, 14-16 minutes.
Whisk the yogurt with the egg, then slowly whisk in a ladle of the hot liquid to temper it before stirring back into the pot.
Warm gently without boiling, then stir in the cilantro and tarragon - dovga, a tangy yogurt and herb soup, is a beloved Azerbaijani dish, sometimes served hot and sometimes chilled in summer.

name: Azerbaijani Piti

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps: Combine the lamb, chickpeas, potato, and onion in a pot with 2 cups water.
Simmer, covered, 1-1.25 hours until everything is tender.
Serve with bread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps_detailed: Combine the lamb, chickpeas, potato, onion, turmeric, and dates in a pot with 2.25 cups water.
Simmer, covered, 1.25-1.5 hours until everything is completely tender - piti is traditionally cooked in individual clay pots at a very low simmer for hours.
Serve with bread, mashing the solids together if you like - piti is a beloved Azerbaijani specialty, especially from the city of Sheki.

name: Azerbaijani Badimjan (Eggplant Stew)
steps: Saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the eggplant and tomatoes, and simmer 20-25 minutes until tender.
Serve with rice or bread.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion and garlic in the oil until softened, 5-6 minutes.
Add the eggplant and diced tomatoes, and simmer, covered, 22-28 minutes until the eggplant is completely tender.
Top with cilantro and serve with rice or bread - badimjan, a comforting eggplant stew, is a common Azerbaijani home-cooked meal.

name: Azerbaijani Dolma

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Mix the raw rice with the lamb for the filling.
Wrap small portions in the grape leaves, rolling tightly.
Simmer in water to cover for 40-45 minutes, and serve with the yogurt.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Mix the raw rice with the lamb, cilantro, and cinnamon for the filling.
Lay each grape leaf flat, place a spoonful of filling near the stem end, fold in the sides, and roll tightly into a small, delicate cylinder.
Arrange the rolls snugly in a pot, cover with water, and simmer 42-50 minutes until the rice and lamb are fully cooked.
Serve with the yogurt - Azerbaijani dolma is traditionally rolled smaller and more delicately than in some neighboring countries.

name: Azerbaijani Plov

ingredients.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Brown the chicken, then add 1.5 cups water and simmer 15 minutes.
Add the rice and simmer, covered, 18-20 minutes until tender.
Fold in the raisins and butter before serving.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Brown the chicken, then add 1.75 cups water and the saffron, and simmer 15-18 minutes.
Add the rice, cover, and simmer 20-25 minutes until tender and fragrant.
Fold in the raisins and butter, and top with the walnuts before serving - plov, saffron rice with meat and dried fruit, is Azerbaijan's most iconic dish, with dozens of regional variations.

name: Azerbaijani Lavangi
steps: Saute the onion until deeply golden, then mix with the walnuts for a filling.
Stuff the fish with the filling and bake at 375F for 20-25 minutes until cooked through.
Top with the pomegranate seeds.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion and garlic until deeply golden and caramelized, 10-12 minutes, then mix with the walnuts and cilantro for a rich filling.
Stuff the fish generously with the filling and bake at 375F (190C) for 22-28 minutes until cooked through and flaky.
Top with the pomegranate seeds - lavangi, walnut-stuffed fish or poultry, is a treasured Azerbaijani dish, especially from the Lankaran region.

name: Azerbaijani Kabab
steps: Mix the lamb with the grated onion, pepper, and lime juice.
Shape into flattened logs and grill until well charred and cooked through.
Serve with flatbread and grilled tomatoes.
steps_detailed: Mix the lamb thoroughly with the grated onion, pepper, lime juice, and cilantro, and let marinate at least 20 minutes.
Shape into flattened logs around skewers if you have them, and grill over medium-high heat, 4-6 minutes per side, until well charred.
Serve with flatbread and the tomatoes - kabab is a beloved Azerbaijani dish, central to gatherings and picnics.

name: Azerbaijani Saj

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Stir-fry the beef, bell pepper, onion, and eggplant together in a hot, wide pan until everything is browned and tender, about 12-15 minutes.
Serve with flatbread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Stir-fry the beef, bell pepper, onion, eggplant, and garlic together in the oil in a hot, wide pan (traditionally a saj, a convex metal griddle), stirring frequently, 14-18 minutes until everything is browned and tender.
Serve with flatbread straight from the pan - saj, a mixed grill of meat and vegetables, is a festive, shared Azerbaijani dish.

name: Azerbaijani Qutab with Meat
steps: Make a thin dough from the flour and water.
Brown the beef with the onion, fill the dough, fold in half, and cook on a hot, dry griddle until browned.
Brush with the oil before serving.
steps_detailed: Make a thin, elastic dough from the flour and water, and roll into a thin round.
Brown the beef with the onion and paprika, spread over half the round, and fold over into a half-moon, sealing the edge.
Cook on a hot, dry griddle, 2-4 minutes per side, until browned and cooked through.
Brush with the oil before serving - a hearty, beloved Azerbaijani street food.

name: Azerbaijani Hummus with Sumac

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, and lime juice with a splash of water until smooth.
Dust with sumac and serve with flatbread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, and garlic with a splash of water until very smooth.
Dust generously with sumac and serve with warm flatbread.

name: Azerbaijani Walnut and Herb Bites
steps: Combine the walnuts, cilantro, and garlic.
Serve as a simple, savory snack.
steps_detailed: Combine the walnuts, cilantro, garlic, and lime juice.
Walnuts and fresh herbs together are a common Azerbaijani flavor pairing, echoed in dishes like lavangi.

name: Azerbaijani Turshu (Pickled Vegetables)
steps: Combine the cabbage, carrot, vinegar, and garlic.
Let sit at least 20 minutes to pickle before serving.
steps_detailed: Combine the cabbage, carrot, vinegar, garlic, and tarragon.
Let sit at least 25-30 minutes so the vegetables lightly pickle - turshu, tangy pickled vegetables, are a staple Azerbaijani table condiment.

name: Azerbaijani Pakhlava
steps: Layer the phyllo with the melted butter, scattering the chopped walnuts between layers.
Cut into diamond shapes and bake at 350F for 25-30 minutes until deeply golden.
Warm the honey with the saffron and pour over while still warm.
steps_detailed: Layer sheets of phyllo in a small buttered dish, brushing each with melted butter, scattering the walnuts mixed with cardamom between layers.
Cut into the traditional diamond shapes before baking.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 28-35 minutes until deeply golden and crisp.
Warm the honey with the saffron and pour evenly over the hot pakhlava - Azerbaijani pakhlava, with its distinctive diamond shape, is especially associated with Novruz, the Persian New Year.

name: Azerbaijani Shekerbura
steps: Make a soft dough from the flour, butter, and water.
Mix the ground walnuts with the sugar for the filling.
Fill small rounds of dough, fold into crescents, crimp the edges, and bake at 350F for 20-25 minutes until pale golden.
steps_detailed: Make a soft, elastic dough from the flour, butter, and water.
Mix the ground walnuts with the sugar and cardamom for the filling.
Roll small rounds of dough, fill with the walnut mixture, fold into crescents, and crimp the edges into the traditional decorative pattern.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 22-28 minutes until just pale golden - shekerbura, meaning 'sugar pastry,' is another beloved Novruz sweet, symbolizing the moon.

name: Azerbaijani Sheki Halva
steps: Cook the noodles until tender, then toss with the honey and saffron.
Layer in a small dish with the walnuts, pressing firmly.
Chill before slicing.
steps_detailed: Cook the noodles (standing in for the fine vermicelli traditionally used) until tender, then toss with the honey, saffron, and melted butter.
Layer in a small buttered dish with the chopped walnuts, pressing down firmly between layers.
Chill at least 1 hour before slicing into diamond shapes - Sheki halva, a distinctive layered noodle and nut sweet, is a specialty of the city of Sheki, wrapped decoratively for special occasions.

name: Azerbaijani Narsharab
steps: Simmer the pomegranate juice with the sugar for 10-12 minutes until reduced and syrupy.
Serve alongside grilled meats and fish.
steps_detailed: Simmer the pomegranate juice with the sugar over medium-low heat for 12-15 minutes until reduced by about half and syrupy, finishing with the lime juice.
Narsharab, a tangy-sweet pomegranate reduction, is a foundational Azerbaijani condiment, essential alongside kabab and lavangi.

name: Azerbaijani Yogurt Herb Sauce

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Whisk the yogurt with the cilantro and garlic.
Season with salt and serve alongside kabab or qutab.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Whisk the yogurt with the cilantro, garlic, and dill until smooth.
Season with salt and chill briefly - a cooling, herb-flecked yogurt sauce common across Azerbaijani cooking.
